contents.title: KOI Protocol: Bridge Research & Knowledge Organization Infrastructure
contents.content:
# KOI Protocol: Bridge Research & Knowledge Organization Infrastructure

**Date**: May 12, 2026
**Lead Author**: @sayertindall (Sayer Tindall)
**Contributors**: @davidfsol5 (David Sisson), @rororowyourboat (Rohan Mehta), @BSCI-Steve (Stephen Zargham)
**Domain**: R&D / Software Development

## Overview
This document outlines the architecture for the newly proposed Knowledge Organization Infrastructure (KOI) bridge, connecting BlockScience's internal knowledge graphs to client-facing research deliverables. As discussed in our [recent Slack thread](https://blockscience.slack.com/archives/C1A2B3C4D/p1712948293), the goal is to formalize the semantic relationships between raw model outputs and our ethnographic research.

## Systems Engineering Context
Building on the quality assurance practices established by **Steve Zargham**, this bridge requires high-reliability data pipelines. **David Sisson** has proposed a distributed data mesh architecture to handle the incoming metadata streams without blocking the primary simulation workers.

Simultaneously, **Rohan Mehta** is developing the LLM information retrieval pipeline that will ingest these KOI artifacts to populate our vector stores.

## Protocol Specifications
1. **Entity Ingestion**: Automatically pulls markdown headers and code annotations from the `bsci-koi-bridge-demo` repository.
2. **Metadata Tagging**: Leverages the expertise tagging system (e.g., *Modeling & Simulation*, *Ethnography*, *Systems Engineering*).
3. **Graph Validation**: We will rely on cadCAD models to simulate the economic viability of the curation market within the KOI protocol.

## Links & References
* **Simulation Repo**: [github.com/BlockScience/bsci-koi-bridge-demo](https://github.com/BlockScience/bsci-koi-bridge-demo)
* **Raw Testing Data**: [GDrive: KOI Network Simulation Outputs (Q2 2026)](#)
* **Governance Integrations**: See Kelsie's notes on [Governance Mechanisms & Ethnographic Insights](governance-mechanisms-ethnography-dao.md)
